text.title: Entry Point, Config, Graceful Shutdown, DI by Hand
text.summary: Thin cmd/api/main.go pattern, env-based config, graceful shutdown with context timeout, and manual dependency wiring.

rule.statement: Keep cmd/<bin>/main.go thin: parse flags, load config, wire deps, call Run(ctx); all logic lives in internal packages.
rule.rationale: A fat main.go is untestable. Thin main with explicit wiring makes every dependency visible, swap-able in tests, and documented by its constructor call.

rule.statement: Load all configuration from environment variables in a Config struct at startup; use os.Getenv with explicit defaults, not dotenv in production.
rule.rationale: dotenv overrides env vars set by the orchestrator. In containerised deployments, env vars come from the platform secrets layer; dotenv belongs in local dev only.

rule.statement: Introduce wire or fx only when hand-wiring in main.go exceeds ~100 lines; resist adding it for small services.
rule.rationale: DI frameworks add a compile step and learning overhead. For fewer than 10 constructors, explicit wiring in main.go is clearer and easier to trace.
